text: Hello and welcome to 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Phil and today we have Hannah joining us again from BBC podcast What In The World.
zh: 我是Phil，今天我们再次请来了BBC播客《What In The World》的Hannah。

text: Welcome, Hannah.
zh: 欢迎你，Hannah。

text: Hey Phil, it is so great to be back here.
zh: 嗨，Phil，很高兴能再次来到这里。

text: So, I'm wondering, do you do things that you think are good for the planet?
zh: 我在想，你会做一些你认为对地球有益的事情吗？

text: Oh, I try, but I don't think I do enough.
zh: 哦，我尽量做，但我觉得做得还不够。

text: So maybe I cycle more than I drive and I try to recycle things, but I should be doing a lot more.
zh: 比如我骑自行车比开车多，也尽量做回收利用，但我应该做得更多。

text: But some people think that to deal with climate change, we can't just rely on individuals.
zh: 不过有些人认为，要应对气候变化，我们不能只靠个人的努力。

text: Phil, it's not up to you and me.
zh: Phil，这不是你我能解决的问题。

text: We need to actually change the way our economy works.
zh: 我们需要真正改变我们经济运作的方式。

text: And this is an idea called degrowth, and we've been investigating it at What In The World, and we're going to learn a bit more about it today.
zh: 这就是所谓的“去增长”理念，我们在《What In The World》节目中一直在研究它，今天我们也来多了解一下。

text: But first, our quiz question.
zh: 不过首先，是我们的题目时间。

text: World leaders promised in 2015 to limit global temperature rises, but what was the limit they agreed to?
zh: 2015年，世界各国领导人承诺限制全球气温上升，那么他们商定的上限是多少？

text: Was it a 1 .5 degrees Celsius, b 2 .5 degrees Celsius, or c 3 .5 degrees Celsius?
zh: 是a. 1.5摄氏度，b. 2.5摄氏度，还是c. 3.5摄氏度？

text: I think I know this one, Phil.
zh: Phil，我觉得我知道这个答案。

text: I think it's 1 .5 degrees Celsius.
zh: 我认为是1.5摄氏度。

text: OK, we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: 好的，我们稍后在节目中揭晓答案。

text: So, Hannah, you've been researching degrowth.
zh: 那么，Hannah，你一直在研究“去增长”这个概念。

text: What does that mean?
zh: 这到底是什么意思？

text: Well, Phil, traditionally governments have always wanted to grow the economy.
zh: 嗯，Phil，传统上各国政府一直希望经济增长。

text: More money, more jobs, more stuff.
zh: 更多的钱，更多的工作，更多的物质产品。

text: But degrowth is a different way of thinking about things.
zh: 但“去增长”是一种不同的思考方式。

text: Some people say that to tackle the climate crisis, we need to stop our economies from growing.
zh: 有些人认为，要应对气候危机，我们需要停止让经济继续增长。

text: We need to stop using and making more and focus on using fewer of the Earth's natural resources.
zh: 我们需要停止过度使用和生产，转而专注于减少对地球自然资源的消耗。

text: The people who support the idea of degrowth are often called degrowthers.
zh: 支持“去增长”理念的人通常被称为“去增长主义者”。

text: Right.
zh: 对。

text: OK, so degrowthers argue we should make and use less stuff.
zh: 所以“去增长主义者”认为我们应该少生产、少消费。

text: Yes, mostly.
zh: 是的，大体如此。

text: It's a little bit more complex than that.
zh: 不过实际情况要复杂一些。

text: And I spoke to BBC journalist Álvaro Álvarez, who told me more about the idea.
zh: 我采访了BBC记者Álvaro Álvarez，他向我详细介绍了这个理念。

text: So, degrowth is not saying that we need to reduce everything.
zh: “去增长”并不是说我们需要减少所有东西。

text: It's saying that we need to decrease some elements of our production that are not beneficial.
zh: 而是说，我们需要减少那些没有益处的生产要素。

text: An example of those elements would be fast fashion, private jets, the beef and meat industry.
zh: 比如快时尚、私人飞机、牛肉和肉类产业。

text: So it's not about decreasing everything.
zh: 所以并不是要减少一切。

text: Alvaro says that de -growthers believe we should reduce some elements of production.
zh: Alvaro说“去增长主义者”认为我们应该减少生产中的某些要素。

text: An element of something is a part of something.
zh: “element”（要素）指的是某事物的一部分。

text: And according to Alvaro, these elements of production are not believed to be beneficial.
zh: 据Alvaro所说，这些生产要素被认为是没有益处的。

text: If something is beneficial, it's useful or good.
zh: 如果某样东西“beneficial”，那就是说它有用或有益。

text: One example of a kind of production that de -growthers disagree with is fast fashion.
zh: “去增长主义者”反对的一种生产类型的例子就是快时尚。

text: Fast fashion refers to clothes that are made very cheaply and quickly, so that customers can afford to buy new outfits more often.
zh: “快时尚”指的是那些生产得非常廉价、迅速的服装，好让顾客能更频繁地购买新衣服。

text: Exactly.
zh: 没错。

text: And before we go any further, many economists do not think that degrowth is a good idea.
zh: 在我们继续之前，需要说明的是，很多经济学家并不认为“去增长”是个好主意。

text: Some of them argue that it would create more poverty, and some economists believe degrowth would actually make it harder to solve the climate crisis.
zh: 其中一些人认为这会加剧贫困，还有些经济学家认为“去增长”反而会让解决气候危机变得更困难。

text: For example, Professor Sam Fankhauser, an economist at Cambridge University in the UK, spoke to you on What in the World?
zh: 例如，英国剑桥大学的经济学家Sam Fankhauser教授在《What In The World》节目中就谈到了这一点。

text: And he argues that degrowth could limit innovation.
zh: 他认为“去增长”可能会限制创新。

text: I think degrowth underestimates the power of innovation.
zh: 我认为“去增长”低估了创新的力量。

text: Degrowth underestimates the need for investment that we need in new technology and in new solutions.
zh: “去增长”低估了我们在新技术和新解决方案上所需要的投资。

text: and degrowth underestimates the social sort of feasibility of what they propose.
zh: 而且“去增长”也低估了他们所提议方案的社会可行性。

text: So Professor Fankhauser thinks that degrowth underestimates many things, such as innovation.
zh: 所以Fankhauser教授认为“去增长”低估了很多方面，比如创新。

text: If you underestimate something, you don't understand the true importance or size of it.
zh: 如果你“underestimate”某事，就是说你没有真正理解它的重要性或规模。

text: Another factor that Professor Fankhauser says is underestimated by degrowthers is the feasibility of social change.
zh: Fankhauser教授提到的另一个被“去增长主义者”低估的因素是社会变革的可行性。

text: Feasibility means the possibility that something can be achieved.
zh: “Feasibility”（可行性）指的是某事能够实现的可能性。

text: It comes from the adjective feasible.
zh: 它来自形容词“feasible”（可行的）。

text: For example, I could say that I think it's feasible for us to finish this podcast in six minutes.
zh: 比如，我可以说我认为我们能在六分钟内完成这期播客是可行的。

text: I think we can do it.
zh: 我觉得我们能做到。

text: Well, Phil, we're going to have to get a move on.
zh: 好了，Phil，我们得抓紧时间了。

text: I think it's time to hear the answer to your quiz question.
zh: 我想现在是时候公布你那道题的答案了。

text: I asked how much world leaders agreed to limit global temperature rises to?
zh: 我问的是，世界领导人商定将全球气温上升限制在多少度以内？

text: The answer is 1 .5 degrees Celsius, which scientists believe is needed to stop the worst impacts of climate change.
zh: 答案是1.5摄氏度，科学家认为这是阻止气候变化最严重影响所必需的界限。

text: I got it right!
zh: 我答对了！

text: OK, so let's recap some of the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with degrowth, which is the economic idea that we should use and make less stuff.
zh: 好的，让我们来回顾一下今天学到的一些词汇，首先是“degrowth”（去增长），指的是我们应该减少使用和生产物质产品的经济理念。

text: An element of something means a part of something.
zh: “element”指的是某事物的一部分。

text: Beneficial means useful or good.
zh: “beneficial”意思是有用的或有益的。

text: Fast fashion refers to clothes that are made cheaply and quickly so that people can buy them more often.
zh: “fast fashion”（快时尚）指的是生产廉价、迅速的服装，好让人们更频繁地购买。

text: And if you underestimate something, you don't understand the true importance or size of it.
zh: 如果你“underestimate”某事，就是说你没有真正理解它的重要性或规模。

text: The feasibility of something is the likelihood that it can be achieved.
zh: 某事物的“feasibility”指的是它能够实现的可能性。

text: We can also describe something as feasible or unfeasible.
zh: 我们也可以说某事是“feasible”（可行的）或“unfeasible”（不可行的）。

text: We can do it, or we can't do it.
zh: 我们能做到，或者我们做不到。

text: Phil, once again our six minutes are up, up, but there is just a little bit more time for me to tell you more about my podcast, What In The World.
zh: Phil，我们的六分钟又到了，不过还有一点时间让我再跟大家说说我的播客《What In The World》。

text: We have a new episode every single weekday and we talk about the news, trending topics.
zh: 我们每个工作日都会更新一期节目，讨论新闻和热门话题。

text: In fact, it's a great place to come and learn some new vocabulary about things that are happening in the world.
zh: 事实上，这是一个学习世界时事相关新词汇的好地方。

text: Yes, if you've found this episode interesting, try watching What In The World on YouTube with subtitles to practise your English.
zh: 没错，如果你觉得这期节目有意思，可以去YouTube上观看《What In The World》并打开字幕，来练习你的英语。

text: There's a link in the notes for this episode.
zh: 这期节目的说明中会附上链接。

text: And And don't forget to try the quiz and worksheet at bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 别忘了到bbclearningenglish.com上试做测验和练习题。

text: Phil, thank you so much for having me again on 6 Minute English.
zh: Phil，非常感谢你再次邀请我来到《六分钟英语》。

text: Bye for now.
zh: 再见啦。

text: Bye.
zh: 再见。
